From [eating] the produce of the fields. Because of the roots and grass that they gathered and ate, their feces increased and they were loathsome.

verses.hebrew_full: יְדֵ֗י נָשִׁים֙ רַחֲמָ֣נִיּ֔וֹת בִּשְּׁל֖וּ יַלְדֵיהֶ֑ן הָי֤וּ לְבָרוֹת֙ לָ֔מוֹ בְּשֶׁ֖בֶר בַּת־עַמִּֽי
verses.translation: י With their own hands, tenderhearted womenHave cooked their children;Such became their fare,In the disaster of my poor people.
verses.rashi: Their food. For food, as in, “and he did not eat בָרָה food with them,”15II Shmuel 12:17.  [and as in] “to serve Dovid a meal לְהַבְרוֹת.”16Ibid. 3:35.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּלָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־חֲמָת֔וֹ שָׁפַ֖ךְ חֲר֣וֹן אַפּ֑וֹ וַיַּצֶּת־אֵ֣שׁ בְּצִיּ֔וֹן וַתֹּ֖אכַל יְסֹדֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: כ GOD gave full vent to fury,And poured out blazing wrath—Kindling a fire in ZionThat consumed its foundations.
verses.rashi: Adonoy spent His wrath. Which burned in Him for many years.17God’s fury is compared to a ‘consuming fire’ as in Eichah 2:3 above; also see Devorim 4:24.  He now spent it when He avenged Himself upon them.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·heat

verses.hebrew_full: לֹ֤א הֶאֱמִ֙ינוּ֙ מַלְכֵי־אֶ֔רֶץ (וכל) כֹּ֖ל יֹשְׁבֵ֣י תֵבֵ֑ל כִּ֤י יָבֹא֙ צַ֣ר וְאוֹיֵ֔ב בְּשַׁעֲרֵ֖י יְרוּשָׁלִָֽם
verses.translation: ל The kings of the earth did not believe,Nor any of the inhabitants of the world,That foe or adversary could enterThe gates of Jerusalem.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵֽחַטֹּ֣אות נְבִיאֶ֔יהָ עֲוֺנֹ֖ת כֹּהֲנֶ֑יהָ הַשֹּׁפְכִ֥ים בְּקִרְבָּ֖הּ דַּ֥ם צַדִּיקִֽים
verses.translation: מ It was for the sins of her prophets,The iniquities of her priests,Who had shed in her midstThe blood of the just.
verses.rashi: For the sins of her false prophets. [Because of the] false [prophets], this evil has befallen her.18Alternately, ‘because of the sins committed against the prophets,’ namely the murder of Zecharyah, this evil has befallen her. (Eichah Rabbah 4:16) Or ‘because of the sins committed by her prophets and kohanim who burnt incense for idol worship, this evil has befallen her.’ (Targum)

verses.hebrew_full: נָע֤וּ עִוְרִים֙ בַּֽחוּצ֔וֹת נְגֹֽאֲל֖וּ בַּדָּ֑ם בְּלֹ֣א יֽוּכְל֔וּ יִגְּע֖וּ בִּלְבֻשֵׁיהֶֽם
verses.translation: נ They wandered blindly through the streets,Defiled with blood,So that no one was ableTo touch their garments.
verses.rashi: The blind slithered in the streets. When the blind would walk in the street, they would stagger, and their feet would slip on the blood of the slain,19Alternatively, ‘they wandered through the streets blindly.’  whom the wicked were slaying in its [Yerusholayim] midst. Filthied with blood. They were so sullied with blood that those near them could not touch their clothes, and would therefore call out to them.20“Get away, you are contaminated, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: ס֣וּרוּ טָמֵ֞א קָ֣רְאוּ לָ֗מוֹ ס֤וּרוּ ס֙וּרוּ֙ אַל־תִּגָּ֔עוּ כִּ֥י נָצ֖וּ גַּם־נָ֑עוּ אָֽמְרוּ֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖פוּ לָגֽוּר
verses.translation: ס “Away! Unclean!” people shouted at them,“Away! Away! Touch not!”So they wandered and wandered again;For the nations had resolved:“They shall stay here no longer.”
verses.rashi: Get away. From us, you unclean ones, who are sullied with blood. For they were obscene. An expression of foulness and filth,21Alternatively, ‘they blasphemed.[נצונאצו].’  as in, “its crop מֻרְאָתוֹ with its feathers,”22Vayikra 1:16.  which is translated [by the Targum] as בְּאוּכְלֵיהּ. So did Menachem classify it. And slithering. They slipped in blood..

verses.hebrew_full: פְּנֵ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ חִלְּקָ֔ם לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖יף לְהַבִּיטָ֑ם פְּנֵ֤י כֹהֲנִים֙ לֹ֣א נָשָׂ֔אוּ (זקנים) וּזְקֵנִ֖ים לֹ֥א חָנָֽנוּ
verses.translation: פ GOD’s countenance has turned away from them, And will look on them no more.They showed no regard for priests,No favor to elders.
verses.rashi: In Adonoy’s glaring anger He dispersed them. The angry face of the Holy One, Blessed Is He, divided and separated them among the nations, because they did not respect the presence of the kohanim when they were in their tranquility.

verses.hebrew_full: (עודינה) עוֹדֵ֙ינוּ֙ תִּכְלֶ֣ינָה עֵינֵ֔ינוּ אֶל־עֶזְרָתֵ֖נוּ הָ֑בֶל בְּצִפִּיָּתֵ֣נוּ צִפִּ֔ינוּ אֶל־גּ֖וֹי לֹ֥א יוֹשִֽׁעַ
verses.translation: ע Even now our eyes pine awayIn vain for deliverance.As we waited, still we waitFor a nation that cannot help.
verses.rashi: We persisted in longingly eyeing illusive reinforcements. When the evil befell us, our eyes were still looking forward to Pharaoh’s army, concerning whom it is stated, “And the Egyptians, they help in vain and to no purpose,”23Yeshayahu 30:7.  for they would promise us aid but they would not come, as it is stated concerning them, “Behold, Pharaoh’s army, which has come out to help [you], is returning to its land, to Egypt.”24Yirmiyahu 37:7.  We find in Midrash Kinos that they were coming in ships. The Holy One, Blessed Is He, hinted to the sea and caused inflated flasks25The text in the Midrash is שלדותיהן, i.e., their skeletons.  like human intestines to move about in the water. They said to each other, “These flasks are our forefathers, the men of Egypt who drowned in the sea on account of these Jews, and we are gong out to assist them?” They stopped and turned back. Yearning. We waited.

verses.hebrew_full: צָד֣וּ צְעָדֵ֔ינוּ מִלֶּ֖כֶת בִּרְחֹבֹתֵ֑ינוּ קָרַ֥ב קִצֵּ֛נוּ מָלְא֥וּ יָמֵ֖ינוּ כִּי־בָ֥א קִצֵּֽנוּ
verses.translation: צ Our steps were checked,We could not walk in our squares. Our doom is near, our days are done—Alas, our doom has come!
verses.rashi: They lurked behind our steps. Our enemies stalked our footsteps [and prevented us] from walking in our streets, as in, “but for one who had not lain in ambush צָדָה”26Shemos 21:13.  [and as in,] “but you are stalking צָדָה my soul.”27I Shmuel 34:12.

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: קַלִּ֤ים הָיוּ֙ רֹדְפֵ֔ינוּ מִנִּשְׁרֵ֖י שָׁמָ֑יִם עַל־הֶהָרִ֣ים דְּלָקֻ֔נוּ בַּמִּדְבָּ֖ר אָ֥רְבוּ לָֽנוּ
verses.translation: ק Our pursuers were swifterThan the eagles in the sky;They chased us in the mountains,Lay in wait for us in the wilderness.

verses.hebrew_full: ר֤וּחַ אַפֵּ֙ינוּ֙ מְשִׁ֣יחַ יְהֹוָ֔ה נִלְכַּ֖ד בִּשְׁחִיתוֹתָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַ֔רְנוּ בְּצִלּ֖וֹ נִֽחְיֶ֥ה בַגּוֹיִֽם
verses.translation: ר The breath of our life, GOD’s anointed, Was captured in their traps—He in whose shade we had thoughtTo live among the nations.
verses.rashi: Adonoy’s anointed. That is Yoshiyahu, as it is stated in Divrei Hayomim, “And Yirmiyahu lamented over Yoshiyahu.”28II Divrei Hayomim 35:25. See Maseches Ta’anis 22b. Alternatively, ‘Adonoy’s appointed’ refers to Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah who was blinded and carried off to Bavel in chains by the people of Kasdim. In their pitfalls. In the pits that they dug.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂ֤ישִׂי וְשִׂמְחִי֙ בַּת־אֱד֔וֹם (יושבתי) יוֹשֶׁ֖בֶת בְּאֶ֣רֶץ ע֑וּץ גַּם־עָלַ֙יִךְ֙ תַּעֲבׇר־כּ֔וֹס תִּשְׁכְּרִ֖י וְתִתְעָרִֽי
verses.translation: ש Rejoice and exult, Fair Edom,Who dwell in the land of Uz!To you, too, the cup shall pass,You shall get drunk and expose your nakedness.
verses.rashi: Rejoice in gladness city of Edom. Yirmiyahu prophesied about the destruction of the second Beis Hamikdosh which the Romans would destroy.29Alternatively, it refers to Edom who rejoiced at the downfall of Yerusholayim and rejoiced at the destruction of the first Beis Hamikdosh, as it says “Remember, Adonoy, for the offspring of Edom...who say ‘Destroy! Destroy!’ etc., in Tehilim 137:7. Rejoice in gladness. For the time being, but ultimately, the cup of retribution will pass also over you, and you will become drunk from it. [Till] you vomit. And you will throw up, as in, “and she emptied וַתְּעַר her pitcher.”30Bereishis 24:20.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through·n f
to be or become drunk or drunken

verses.hebrew_full: תַּם־עֲוֺנֵךְ֙ בַּת־צִיּ֔וֹן לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖יף לְהַגְלוֹתֵ֑ךְ פָּקַ֤ד עֲוֺנֵךְ֙ בַּת־אֱד֔וֹם גִּלָּ֖ה עַל־חַטֹּאתָֽיִךְ
verses.translation: ת Your iniquity, Fair Zion, is expiated;You will be exiled no longer.Your iniquity, Fair Edom, [God] will note,And uncover your sins.
verses.rashi: You have been fully chastised for your sins city of Tzion. You suffered for all your sins. He will never exile you again. After the exile of Edom.

verses.hebrew_full: זְכֹ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ מֶֽה־הָ֣יָה לָ֔נוּ (הביט) הַבִּ֖יטָה וּרְאֵ֥ה אֶת־חֶרְפָּתֵֽנוּ
verses.translation: Remember, O ETERNAL One, what has befallen us;Behold, and see our disgrace!

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·reproach

verses.hebrew_full: נַחֲלָתֵ֙נוּ֙ נֶֽהֶפְכָ֣ה לְזָרִ֔ים בָּתֵּ֖ינוּ לְנׇכְרִֽים
verses.translation: Our heritage has passed to aliens,Our homes to strangers.

verses.hebrew_full: יְתוֹמִ֤ים הָיִ֙ינוּ֙ (אין) וְאֵ֣ין אָ֔ב אִמֹּתֵ֖ינוּ כְּאַלְמָנֽוֹת
verses.translation: We have become orphans, fatherless;Our mothers are like widows.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵימֵ֙ינוּ֙ בְּכֶ֣סֶף שָׁתִ֔ינוּ עֵצֵ֖ינוּ בִּמְחִ֥יר יָבֹֽאוּ
verses.translation: We must pay to drink our own water,Obtain our own kindling at a price.
verses.rashi: We pay to drink our own water. Since we were afraid to draw water from the river because of the enemies, we bought [water] from them with money.1Alternatively, ‘we are accustomed to drink from silver cups, and now we even have to pay to drink from wooden cups. (Yalkut Me’am Lo’ez)

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֤ל צַוָּארֵ֙נוּ֙ נִרְדָּ֔פְנוּ יָגַ֖עְנוּ (לא) וְלֹ֥א הֽוּנַֽח־לָֽנוּ
verses.translation: We are hotly pursued;Exhausted, we are given no rest.
verses.rashi: We are hounded around our necks. Because of the yoke of hard labor. We exhaust ourselves. To gather money and property. But nothing remains for us. [Of] our toil in our hands, because the enemies would collect and seize everything for taxes, head taxes, and property taxes.2Alternatively, ‘and we were given no rest [ולא הונח לנו].’

words.eng: to rest·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to us’.

verses.hebrew_full: מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ נָתַ֣נּוּ יָ֔ד אַשּׁ֖וּר לִשְׂבֹּ֥עַֽ לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: We hold out a hand to Egypt;To Assyria, for our fill of bread.
verses.rashi: Toward Egypt we extended a hand [for assistance]. It is customary for a person who is falling and wishes to stand up to stretch out a hand to someone nearby to help him. Here too, we stretched out a hand to Egypt that they should help us.3Alternatively, Egypt gave us their hand[shake], i.e., an expression of making an oath, that they would assist us. (Ibn Ezra) [Toward] Ashur. That they sate us with their bread. We extended [a hand]. נָתַנּוּ is] the same as נָתַנְנוּ. The dagesh in the nun takes the place of the second nun, as in, “for all is from You, and from Your hand we have given נָתַנּוּ it to You,”4I Divrei Hayomim 29:14.  and as in, “and we will give וְנָתַנּוּ our daughters to you.”5Bereishis 34:16.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲבֹתֵ֤ינוּ חָֽטְאוּ֙ (אינם) וְאֵינָ֔ם (אנחנו) וַאֲנַ֖חְנוּ עֲוֺנֹתֵיהֶ֥ם סָבָֽלְנוּ
verses.translation: Our ancestors sinned and are no more;And we must bear their guilt.

words.eng: we (first pers. pl. -usually used for emphasis)
we (first pers. pl. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: עֲבָדִים֙ מָ֣שְׁלוּ בָ֔נוּ פֹּרֵ֖ק אֵ֥ין מִיָּדָֽם
verses.translation: Slaves are ruling over us,With none to rescue us from them.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנַפְשֵׁ֙נוּ֙ נָבִ֣יא לַחְמֵ֔נוּ מִפְּנֵ֖י חֶ֥רֶב הַמִּדְבָּֽר
verses.translation: We get our bread at the peril of our lives,Because of the sword of the wilderness.
verses.rashi: We gain our bread at the risk of our lives. With our life in jeopardy. We were in danger when we would bring our food from the field because of the sword of the wilderness.

verses.hebrew_full: עוֹרֵ֙נוּ֙ כְּתַנּ֣וּר נִכְמָ֔רוּ מִפְּנֵ֖י זַלְעֲפ֥וֹת רָעָֽב
verses.translation: Our skin glows like an oven,With the fever of famine.
verses.rashi: Inflamed. Became heated, as in, “for his mercies were aroused נִכְמְרוּ”;6Ibid. 43:30.  and in the language of the Gemara there are many instances, “a heating vessel כּוֹמֶר of grapes”;7Maseches Yevamos 97a.  “the heating מִכְמָר of the flesh.”8Maseches Pesachim 58a. Burning hunger. As in, “and a burning זִלְעָפוֹת wind,”9Tehilim 11:6.  an expression of burning.

verses.hebrew_full: נָשִׁים֙ בְּצִיּ֣וֹן עִנּ֔וּ בְּתֻלֹ֖ת בְּעָרֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה
verses.translation: Those slaves have abused women in Zion,Maidens in the towns of Judah.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׂרִים֙ בְּיָדָ֣ם נִתְל֔וּ פְּנֵ֥י זְקֵנִ֖ים לֹ֥א נֶהְדָּֽרוּ
verses.translation: Princes have been hanged by them;No respect has been shown to elders.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּחוּרִים֙ טְח֣וֹן נָשָׂ֔אוּ וּנְעָרִ֖ים בָּעֵ֥ץ כָּשָֽׁלוּ
verses.translation: Young men must carry millstones,And youths stagger under loads of wood.
verses.rashi: Encumbered with millstones. When the enemies led them away in neck irons, they would place on their shoulders millstones and burdens in order to tire them.10Alternatively, ‘youths were forced to grind wheat,’ a task which drains their strength. (Ibn Ezra)  And similarly, “[and boys] staggered with beams,” [meaning that] their strength failed. The expression of staggering כִּשְׁלוֹן applies to the weakening of strength, as is stated in Ezra, “And Yehudah said, ‘the strength of the bearer has failed,’”11Nechemyah 4:4.  and similarly, “He has caused my strength to fail.”12Eichah 1:14.

verses.hebrew_full: זְקֵנִים֙ מִשַּׁ֣עַר שָׁבָ֔תוּ בַּחוּרִ֖ים מִנְּגִינָתָֽם
verses.translation: The elders are gone from the gate,The youngsters from their music.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁבַת֙ מְשׂ֣וֹשׂ לִבֵּ֔נוּ נֶהְפַּ֥ךְ לְאֵ֖בֶל מְחֹלֵֽנוּ
verses.translation: Gone is the joy of our hearts;Our dancing is turned into mourning.

verses.hebrew_full: נָֽפְלָה֙ עֲטֶ֣רֶת רֹאשֵׁ֔נוּ אֽוֹי־נָ֥א לָ֖נוּ כִּ֥י חָטָֽאנוּ
verses.translation: The crown has fallen from our head;Woe to us that we have sinned!

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־זֶ֗ה הָיָ֤ה דָוֶה֙ לִבֵּ֔נוּ עַל־אֵ֖לֶּה חָשְׁכ֥וּ עֵינֵֽינוּ
verses.translation: Because of this our hearts are sick,Because of these our eyes are dimmed:
verses.rashi: For this our hearts ached, etc. Because of that which is delineated in the verse following, “Over Mount Tzion, which was devastated [and] foxes meandered over it.”13Below, verse 18. Our hearts ache only for the destruction of Mount Tzion, and nothing else. (Palgei Mayim)

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֤ל הַר־צִיּוֹן֙ שֶׁשָּׁמֵ֔ם שׁוּעָלִ֖ים הִלְּכוּ־בֽוֹ
verses.translation: Because of Mount Zion, which lies desolate;Jackals prowl over it.

words.eng: to go·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: אַתָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ לְעוֹלָ֣ם תֵּשֵׁ֔ב כִּסְאֲךָ֖ לְד֥וֹר וָדֽוֹר
verses.translation: But You, O ETERNAL One, are enthroned forever,Your throne endures through the ages.
verses.rashi: Adonoy, You. We know that You will remain forever. And because that is so...

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֤מָּה לָנֶ֙צַח֙ תִּשְׁכָּחֵ֔נוּ תַּֽעַזְבֵ֖נוּ לְאֹ֥רֶךְ יָמִֽים
verses.translation: Why have You forgotten us utterly,Forsaken us for all time?
verses.rashi: Why [then O Eternal] do You forget us. Have You not sworn to us Yourself that just as You exist, so does Your oath exist?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲשִׁיבֵ֨נוּ יְהֹוָ֤ה אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ (ונשוב) וְֽנָשׁ֔וּבָה חַדֵּ֥שׁ יָמֵ֖ינוּ כְּקֶֽדֶם
verses.translation: Take us back, O ETERNAL One, to Yourself,And let us come back;Renew our days as of old!
verses.ibn_ezra: RETURN US-To the city of the dwelling place (sanctuary) of your name, and we shall return to serving (worshiping ) You as in days of old.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֚י אִם־מָאֹ֣ס מְאַסְתָּ֔נוּ קָצַ֥פְתָּ עָלֵ֖ינוּ עַד־מְאֹֽד
verses.translation: For truly, You have rejected us,Bitterly raged against us.Take us back, O ETERNAL One, to Yourself,And let us come back;Renew our days as of old!
verses.rashi: For although You may have considered us contemptible. Although we have sinned, [nonetheless] You should not have been angered as much as You have been. Adonoy return us. Because he concludes with words of reproof, he had to repeat the preceding verse again,14In order to end on a note of consolation.  and so did Yeshayahu, Trei Osor, and Koheles.
